page: UpCloud GPU-20xCPU-256GB-2xL40S is a GPU-accelerated server built on the KVM hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It features 20 vCPUs mapped to 20 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9575F processor running at 2.0 GHz, utilizing a shared CPU allocation model. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a high resource density of 12.8 GB per core. Graphics and parallel computing acceleration are delivered by two NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L40S GPUs, providing a combined 96 GB of VRAM. This server configuration does not include local storage.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier performance in single-core operations, with stress-ng single-core, geekbench single-core, and text processing scoring in the top 10% of tested systems. Multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth (both read and write) rank in the top 25%. For machine learning workloads, LLM inference speeds for small models (135M) reach top-tier levels for both prompt processing and text generation, while medium models (7B) achieve strong text generation speeds but average prompt processing speeds.

From a resource density perspective, the server offers a high ratio of memory and GPU accelerator capacity relative to its 20 vCPUs. However, the shared CPU allocation represents a potential tradeoff, as multi-core performance may vary under host contention. The absence of local storage requires external storage provisioning, which affects the overall architectural cost. This configuration is suitable for GPU-accelerated workloads such as small-to-medium LLM inference, image processing, and parallelizable cryptographic tasks, as well as memory-intensive database operations.
description: UpCloud GPU-20xCPU-256GB-2xL40S is a GPU-accelerated server featuring an x86_64 AMD EPYC 9575F processor with 20 vCPUs on a shared CPU allocation, paired with 256.0 GB of system memory. The hardware configuration includes two NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L40S GPUs with a total of 96 GB VRAM, but lacks local storage. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong multi-core capabilities, alongside top-tier LLM inference speeds for small models and strong memory bandwidth. While the shared CPU allocation and lack of local storage present architectural tradeoffs, the high memory-to-core ratio of 12.8 GB per core and dual-GPU setup make this server highly suitable for machine learning inference, image processing, parallelizable cryptographic tasks, and memory-intensive database operations.
og_description: UpCloud GPU-20xCPU-256GB-2xL40S is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 20 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB of RAM, and dual NVIDIA L40S GPUs with 96 GB of VRAM, optimized for machine learning and database workloads.
meta_description: UpCloud GPU-20xCPU-256GB-2xL40S is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 20 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB of system memory, and dual NVIDIA L40S GPUs with 96 GB of VRAM.
tagline: A dual-GPU accelerated platform featuring high memory density and top-tier single-core processing for machine learning and parallel computing workloads.
bullet_points: Dual NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L40S GPUs with 96 GB total VRAM
20 AMD EPYC 9575F vCPUs with shared CPU allocation
256 GB system memory providing 12.8 GB per core
Top-tier single-core CPU and small-model LLM inference performance
No local storage included, requiring external storage provisioning
Suited for machine learning inference, image processing, and databases
